notes_for_human_reviewer: Strong submission near ceiling. All five variables explicitly named with current state and quoted evidence. Three silent cuts surfaced (compressed UAT, unbilled overtime, deferred non-core POS) with quantified pressure. Scope disambiguation distinguishes documented scope, in-practice scope, and sponsor's mental model — gets 4 not 5 because the three are mentioned but not deeply traced. All trade-offs made explicit ('schedule pressure is being absorbed into quality and into team capacity'). Long-term cost connections are quantified with rough magnitudes (£50-150k post-launch defects, £100-500k security incident, £200-400k phase 2). Falls slightly short on long_term_cost (4 not 5) — could explicitly recommend a closeout-items list. Should be a clean pass for a calibrated grader.
